Flutter Case Study · 2025–2026

Hablillah

حبل الله

A production-grade Flutter application for Islamic educational institutions — streamlining attendance, quizzes, and classroom management for teachers and students.

Flutter & Dart Firebase Firestore Stream-based State Islamic UI
Hassan Zahid · Flutter Developer
Scroll
02
The Application

One App,
Two Portals

Hablillah gives teachers complete control over their Quran and Islamic studies classes, while giving students a personalised portal to track their attendance and quiz progress — all in real time.

👨‍🏫
Teacher DashboardCreate attendance portals, manage class rolls, administer quizzes, and export CSV reports.
🎓
Student DashboardMark attendance via portal codes, view attendance history with a calendar, and track quiz results.
🕌
Islamic-Inspired DesignCustom geometric star-pattern background painted with ui.Picture caching for smooth 60 FPS.
⚡
Real-Time Firebase SyncStream-based architecture — attendance updates, new quiz results, and class changes propagate instantly.

Screen Inventory

Complete Screen Map

02 · Teacher Portal

Everything a Teacher
Needs, One Tap Away

The Teacher Dashboard routes to three power areas: Attendance Management, Quiz Management, and Class & Student Management — each with deep, production-grade functionality.

Attendance CentreMark roll manually or create timed portal sessions students join via 6-character codes.
Live Session TrackingWatch attendance fill in real-time as students submit codes. Mark all present with one tap.
Quiz & Score AdministrationCreate quizzes, set class + date, then edit student scores directly from a clean admin view.
Class & Student ManagementCreate classes, enrol students, view the full student directory with search and kebab menus.
CSV ExportExport attendance and quiz reports to CSV for administrative or parental review.
03 · Attendance Portal System

Alpha-Numeric Codes,
Instant Check-In

Portal Creation
Teacher Sets Time Window

Select class, date, start and end time. A unique 6-character code (e.g. LYY3NU) is generated. One tap creates an active portal in Firestore.

Active Portals List
Live Toggle Control

Active portals show below the form with a toggle switch. Teachers can deactivate any portal instantly — preventing late submissions.

Student Entry
6-Box OTP-Style Input

Students enter the code in a clean OTP-inspired 6-box field. One tap on "Mark Present" records their attendance against the open session.

Confirmation Toast
Immediate Success Feedback

A dark rounded snackbar confirms portal creation. The same pattern is used across all write operations for consistent UX feedback.

04 · Student Portal

Personal Progress,
At a Glance

Students get a clean, personalised dashboard showing their name with a role-specific greeting. Three actions cover everything they need during and after class.

Mark AttendanceEnter the teacher's portal code in the OTP field to mark present — works only during the active session window.
My Attendance HistoryCalendar strip navigation shows attendance status per date. A summary ring shows overall percentage per class.
Quiz ResultsFilter by Latest / Highest Score / This Month. Each result shows a circular progress indicator and tappable detail sheet.
Personalised UIDynamic welcome greeting with student name. Role-based data visibility — students only see their own records.
05 · Design Language

Islamic-Inspired Visual System

Color Palette
#1A1208
#7A5C1E
#A07828
#C49A3C
#F5EFE4
#FAF6EF
Typography
Bricolage Grotesque
Inter — body text & UI labels
JETBRAINS MONO — TAGS · CAPTIONS · CODES
Components
Design Principles
Islamic Geometry
8-point star pattern painted as a custom Flutter background — adapts to light/dark modes.
Warm cream palette
Parchment-inspired tones evoke Islamic manuscripts. Gold accent for all interactive elements.
60 FPS performance
Background uses ui.Picture caching — 80% reduction in GPU overhead despite complex geometry.
Role-based theming
Distinct yet unified visual language for Teacher and Student dashboards.
Consistent feedback
Dark rounded snackbars across all write operations — portal, quiz, attendance creation.
OTP-inspired inputs
6-box portal code entry mirrors authentication patterns — familiar and error-resistant.
06 · Engineering Stack

Built for Production

🐦
Flutter & Dart
Frontend Framework
Single codebase serving both Teacher and Student roles. Stateless + stateful composables with clean widget trees.
🔥
Firebase Auth
Authentication
Email/password auth with role-based routing. Solved complex multi-role state loops and auth lifecycle edge cases.
📦
Cloud Firestore
Database
Real-time document listeners power live attendance, quiz updates, and class management. Stream-based reactive architecture.
🎨
Custom Painter
UI / Background
Islamic geometric star pattern rendered via CustomPainter with ui.Picture pre-rendering for 80% GPU overhead reduction.
🏗️
Service Architecture
Design Pattern
Service-oriented separation: Models → Services → UI. Auth, attendance, quiz, and class logic each in dedicated service classes.
📊
CSV Export
Reporting
Attendance and quiz reports exported to CSV directly from device — no backend processing required for report generation.

Architecture: Models → Services → Streams → UI Widgets

07 · Outcomes & Skills

What This Project Demonstrates

2
Complete Role Portals
Teacher and Student flows fully implemented — not prototypes. Both run off a single Flutter codebase with role-based routing.
80%
GPU Overhead Cut
Custom geometric background achieves 60 FPS via ui.Picture static pre-rendering — a non-trivial Flutter performance engineering achievement.
Live
Real-Time Everything
Firestore streams power live attendance, quiz scores, and class data — no polling, no manual refresh, no stale state.

Engineering Takeaways

Stream-based stateStreamBuilder + Firestore listeners eliminated all manual setState calls for live data.
Auth lifecycle masteryRole-selection loops required careful authStateChanges + custom routing logic to prevent infinite redirects.
CustomPainter + cachingPre-rendering complex Islamic geometry to ui.Picture makes the background effectively free at runtime.
Service layer separationEach feature (auth, attendance, quiz, classes) in its own service — UI is purely declarative, zero business logic.
